channel.title: Undergraduate Research Scholars on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science
channel.description: Recent content in Undergraduate Research Scholars on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science

item.title: For Faculty
item.description:
For Faculty

The URS program encourages faculty to use URS funding to bring additional research support to their projects. It provides grant funding and payroll infrastructure to compensate undergraduates to work as research assistants on approved projects.

Funding Details

  • Maximum grant: $1,250 per year

How to Participate

Visit the How to Apply section for instructions. After receiving funding, faculty have two options:

  1. Inform IQSS of any Harvard undergraduate(s) they plan to hire, OR
  2. Let IQSS help find a research assistant by posting the opportunity on the Research Opportunities page.

Contact: funding@iq.harvard.edu

item.title: For Students
item.description:
For Students

Harvard undergraduates can participate as paid research assistants to IQSS Faculty Affiliates. Faculty funded by the URS program represent a wide array of schools and disciplines at Harvard.

How to Participate

There are two ways to join:

  1. Check out current job postings for projects needing research assistants.

— OR —

  1. Reach out to a faculty member you’d like to work for, and discuss applying for a URS grant to hire you.

Eligibility

  • Must be a Harvard undergraduate

Benefits

  • Paid position as a research assistant
  • Work with IQSS Faculty Affiliates across multiple Harvard schools and disciplines
  • Gain first-hand research experience

Contact: funding@iq.harvard.edu
